frameworks.name: visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: delay-period storage of orientation under sensory noise and distraction

frameworks.name: memory attraction / adaptive distortion
frameworks.one_line: remembered orientation drifts toward distractor orientation during delay

core_question: Does sensory uncertainty at encoding modulate susceptibility of working memory to distractor interference?
key_assumptions: bandpass filter width titrates orientation information in stimulus
delay-period distractors can interfere with stored memory
single-feature orientation report indexes memory fidelity
manipulations: sensory noise level at encoding (high vs low bandpass width)
distractor condition during delay (none, ignored, attended)
key_findings: higher encoding noise reduced working memory accuracy
oriented distractors interfered with memory and biased reports toward distractor orientation
no interaction between encoding noise and distractor presence
interpretations: working memory representations are vulnerable to adaptive distortions during delay
these delay-period distortions are not modulated by encoding sensory noise
